location: Success Criteria > Measurable Outcomes table, 'Time spent on spec revision' row
description: Signal column for 'Time spent on spec revision' row duplicates the Target column verbatim, making the Signal meaningless for this row.
evidence: Target: 'Decreases >=20% per story cycle from baseline' | Signal: 'Decreases >=20% per story cycle from baseline' — identical text
suggestion: Replace Signal with 'Specs are compounding, not fragmenting' — consistent with the surrounding narrative language.

description: The 'four debt types (verification, cognitive, pattern drift, technical)' are listed once as a parenthetical and never defined, explained, or referenced again in the document. A reader new to Momentum has no basis for understanding what these mean.
evidence: 'without accumulating the four debt types (verification, cognitive, pattern drift, technical)'
suggestion: Add a brief definition of each debt type at first mention, or add a footnote/sidebar. At minimum, one-line descriptors for each type.

location: Journey Requirements Summary table vs Journey 2 narrative
description: Journey 2's requirements paragraph mentions 'flywheel integration with retrospective' and 'measurable improvement across sprints' as requirements revealed, but neither appears as a row in the Journey Requirements Summary table.
evidence: Journey 2 Requirements revealed: '...flywheel integration with retrospective, measurable improvement across sprints.' Journey Requirements Summary table has no 'retrospective integration' or 'measurable improvement' row.
suggestion: Add 'Retrospective integration' as a row in the Journey Requirements Summary, marked J2. Or remove 'flywheel integration with retrospective' from the Journey 2 requirements revealed list if it is not a distinct capability.
